Notifications.OnlyGems: Only socket-compatible items can be inserted.
Notifications.GemIncompatible: That item is not compatible with this socket.
Notifications.CannotOpenGem: Could not open the gem item.
Notifications.GemRequiresSocket: This gem must be socketed before it can be used.
Notifications.HostNotSocketable: This item type cannot receive sockets.
Notifications.SocketConditionFailed: That gem does not meet this socket's requirements.
Notifications.SocketConditionError: This socket condition could not be evaluated.
Notifications.InsufficientSocketGems: Not enough socketed gems to consume ({available}/{required}).
Notifications.SourceGemMissing: The gem that provides this activity is no longer socketed.
Notifications.InvalidConsumptionSlot: The socket slot configured for consumption does not exist.
Notifications.InvalidConsumptionTarget: This socket consumption target is not configured.

GemTargetTypes.Label: Allowed Item Types
GemTargetTypes.Hint: Choose which item subtypes can receive this gem.

GemDetails.Hint: Choose how this gem should be categorized.

ExtraDamage.Label: Extra Damage
ExtraDamage.Hint: Add one or more extra damage entries to rolls made with the item holding this gem, including weapons and spells. These fields are scoped to the host item; actor-level Active Effects remain global. For armor and shields, configure the gem's standard Active Effects on the item instead.
ExtraDamage.FormulaHint: Enable this to enter a roll formula instead of manually choosing number, die, and bonus.
ExtraDamage.TypeHint: Choose one or more damage types. Same as host is exclusive and clears the other selections. If multiple fixed types are selected, the roll dialog lets the player choose one of them.
ExtraDamage.TypeModeHint: Same as host uses the socketed item's rolled damage type. Fixed type(s) lets this gem define one or more damage types for the roll dialog.
ExtraDamage.ActivityHint: Choose when this extra damage applies. Any action: always. Attack only: only on attack-type activities. Spell only: only when socketed into a spell item.

CritDamage.Label: Critical-Only Damage
CritDamage.Hint: Damage that only applies when this gem is socketed and you roll critical damage (button or crit action).

CritThreshold.Label: Critical Threshold
CritThreshold.Hint: Lowest d20 result that counts as a critical hit for attack rolls made with the item holding this gem, including spell attacks. If multiple socketed gems adjust it, the lowest value is used. Unlike an actor-level Active Effect, this applies only to the host item. Leave blank to use the default value.

CritMultiplier.Label: Critical Multiplier
CritMultiplier.Hint: Multiplies critical damage rolled by the item holding this gem, including spells. If multiple socketed gems set it, the highest value is used. Unlike an actor-level Active Effect, this applies only to the host item. Leave blank to keep the default multiplier.

AttackBonus.Label: Attack Bonus
AttackBonus.Hint: Flat bonus added to attack rolls made with the item holding this gem, including spell attacks. Bonuses from multiple socketed gems stack. Unlike an actor-level Active Effect, this applies only to the host item. Leave blank for none.

Resource.Label: Socketed Resource
Resource.Hint: Custom resource this gem provides while socketed (e.g. battery, magic). Activities can consume it through the Socket Consumption block. Leave the key blank to provide none.
Resource.DestroyOnEmptyHint: When a consumption drains this gem to zero charges, the gem is destroyed and removed from its socket.

Tags.Label: Gem Tags
Tags.Tooltip: Add stable identifiers for this gem. Tags are converted to lowercase; spaces and accents are normalized. Socket conditions can check them with gemTags or hasGemTag(tag). Example: return hasGemTag("poison");

ItemDetails.EnableSocketTabHint: Show the Sockets tab on this item when the global display setting is disabled.
ItemDetails.EnableSocketTabLockedHint: Items that already have sockets keep this option enabled until all sockets are removed.

SocketSlotConfig.Title: Socket Slot Settings
SocketSlotConfig.Subtitle: Configure rules, description, and tint for this slot.
SocketSlotConfig.InspectHint: Opens the socketed gem in read-only mode.
SocketSlotConfig.NoGem: No gem is currently socketed in this slot.

Hidden.Label: Hide slot
Hidden.Hint: Only GMs can see this slot and its socket description.

DeleteGemOnRemoval.Label: Delete gem on removal
DeleteGemOnRemoval.Hint: When enabled, this slot deletes its gem when unsocketed even if the global setting is disabled.

Condition.Label: Slot condition
Condition.Hint: Use JavaScript. Gem tags are available through gemTags and hasGemTag(tag). Other variables: gem, gemItem, hostItem, item, actor, user, slot, slotConfig, slotIndex, source, getProperty, hasProperty, deepClone.

Description.Label: Slot description
Description.Hint: Shown in Socket Descriptions while the slot is empty. When a gem is added, the gem description takes over.

Color.Label: Slot color
Color.Hint: Applied only to the empty socket frame.

Validation.InvalidCondition: The slot condition has invalid code.

GemCharges.Label: Gem charges
GemCharges.Hint: Current charges of the socketed gem. The maximum is configured on the gem itself.

Tooltips.RemoveGem: Remove the gem from this socket.
Tooltips.DeleteSocket: Delete this slot from the item.

EditPermission.Name: Edit Socket Permission
EditPermission.Hint: The minimum role required to add or remove sockets from items.

SocketableItemTypes.Name: Socketable Item Types
SocketableItemTypes.Label: Configure Socketable Item Types
SocketableItemTypes.Hint: Choose which item types can receive sockets.
SocketableItemTypes.Title: Socketable Item Types
SocketableItemTypes.SelectLabel: Item types that can receive sockets
SocketableItemTypes.SelectHint: Choose one or more item types.

MaxSockets.Name: Maximum Number of Sockets per Item
MaxSockets.Hint: Maximum number of sockets an item can have. Use -1 for unlimited.

DeleteOnRemoval.Name: Delete Gem on Removal
DeleteOnRemoval.Hint: If enabled, a gem is destroyed when removed from a socket; otherwise it's returned to the player's inventory.

GemLootSubtypes.Name: Gem Loot Subtypes
GemLootSubtypes.Label: Configure Gem Loot Subtypes
GemLootSubtypes.Hint: Select which loot subtypes count as gems and customize the extra subtype label.
GemLootSubtypes.Title: Gem Loot Subtype Settings
GemLootSubtypes.SelectHint: Items with these loot subtypes will be treated as gems.

Selection.Name: Gem Loot Subtypes
Selection.Label: Configure Gem Loot Subtypes
Selection.Hint: Select which loot subtypes count as gems.
Selection.Title: Gem Loot Subtype Settings
Selection.SelectHint: Items with these loot subtypes will be treated as gems.

CustomMenu.Name: Custom Loot Subtypes
CustomMenu.Label: Configure Custom Loot Subtypes
CustomMenu.Hint: Add custom loot subtype keys and labels that can be used as gems.
CustomMenu.Title: Custom Loot Subtypes

Custom.Heading: Custom loot subtypes
Custom.Hint: Add new loot subtype keys and labels that will be available for gems.
Custom.Label: Display label

GemRollLayout.Name: Gem damage layout in roll dialog
GemRollLayout.Hint: Choose how gem damage is organized in the damage roll configuration dialog.

Gem.Label: Group by gem
Gem.Description: Each socketed gem keeps its own block and shows only the damage rows that came from that specific gem.

Type.Label: Group by damage type
Type.Description: All rows are merged by damage type, regardless of which gem or item created them.

GemType.Label: Group by gem type
GemType.Description: Identical gems are merged into one block. The title shows 2x, 3x, and so on when more than one copy contributes.

TypeBadges.Label: Group by damage type with badges
TypeBadges.Description: All rows are merged by damage type and each result shows badges for the gems, plus the host item when its own damage is part of that type.

GemFormulaLayout.Name: Gem damage in the sheet Formula column
GemFormulaLayout.Hint: Choose how the extra damage from socketed gems appears in the Formula column of the character sheet.

Current.Label: Hidden (current behavior)
Current.Description: The Formula column only shows the item's own damage, exactly as before.

Inline.Label: Inline
Inline.Description: Every gem damage formula and its type are listed directly in the Formula column.

Tooltip.Label: Tooltip
Tooltip.Description: The Formula column stays compact; hovering or focusing a small gem badge reveals the full breakdown.

GemFormulaShowImage.Name: Show gem image in the Formula breakdown
GemFormulaShowImage.Hint: If enabled, gems in the Formula breakdown are identified by a small image; otherwise the gem name is used. The tooltip breakdown always shows the image and this option only hides the gem name there.

SocketTabLayout.Name: Socket tab layout
SocketTabLayout.Hint: Choose how the sockets tab is displayed.

EnableSocketTabForAllItems.Name: Enable Socket Tab on all items
EnableSocketTabForAllItems.Hint: If enabled, the Sockets tab appears on every socketable item. If disabled, it must be enabled per item in Details.

SupportMenu.Name: Support the developer
SupportMenu.Label: Open support popup
SupportMenu.Hint: Open the Shattered Codex popup with release notes, module highlights, Patreon, Discord, and wiki links.

DocumentationMenu.Name: Documentation
DocumentationMenu.Label: Open wiki
DocumentationMenu.Hint: Open the SC - Simple Sockets documentation wiki.

SocketBehaviorMenu.Name: Socket settings
SocketBehaviorMenu.Label: Configure socket settings
SocketBehaviorMenu.Hint: Open a dedicated window for socket permissions, limits, gem handling, and layout options.
SocketBehaviorMenu.Description: Manage socket permissions, limits, and display behavior in one place.

Sections.RulesHint: Control who can edit sockets and how gems behave when attached or removed.
Sections.DisplayHint: Adjust how gem damage and the socket tab are presented in the UI.

HideSupportCard.Name: Hide automatic What's New popup until next update
HideSupportCard.Hint: After the What's New popup appears for the current version, this option can keep it hidden until the next update. Uncheck it if you want the popup to appear whenever the world loads.

SupportCard.Description: Support my work and stay up to date with exclusive modules and development progress.
SupportCard.Intro: Support us on Patreon to help us keep creating.
SupportCard.Exclusive: Get access to the exclusive SC - More Gems module with 120+ gems and monthly updates. We are also building SC - Setforge so you can create item sets.
SupportCard.Footer: This chat card will only be shown once. Enable it again in the settings if needed.
SupportCard.Title: What's New
SupportCard.DontShowAgain: Don't show again until the next update

DeleteSocket.Title: Delete Socket
DeleteSocket.Message: Are you sure you want to delete this socket?
DeleteSocket.Hint: Hold Shift to bypass this prompt.

RemoveGem.Title: Remove Gem
RemoveGem.Message: Are you sure you want to remove the gem from this slot?

AddSocket.SelectPrompt: Click an item to add a socket. Press Esc to cancel.
AddSocket.NoPermission: You do not have permission to add sockets.
AddSocket.TargetConditionBody: This item does not meet the socket activity target condition.
AddSocket.TargetConditionErrorBody: The target condition for this socket activity could not be evaluated.
AddSocket.ItemPermissionBody: You do not have permission to edit {name}.
AddSocket.MaxReachedBody: {name} already has {count} socket(s), which is the maximum allowed ({max}).
AddSocket.SelectAnother: Do you want to select another item?
AddSocket.Error: Failed to add socket. See console for details.

ExtractGem.SelectPrompt: Click a filled socket to extract its gem. Press Esc to cancel.
ExtractGem.InvalidSlot: Could not resolve the clicked socket.
ExtractGem.EmptySlot: That slot does not contain a gem.
ExtractGem.ItemPermissionBody: You do not have permission to edit {name}.
ExtractGem.Error: Failed to extract gem. See console for details.

Common.SelectAnother: Do you want to select another item?

CursorImage.Label: Click cursor image
CursorImage.Hint: Optional image path or URL used as the cursor while this activity waits for the next clicked target. Leave blank to use the default hammer cursor.

Warnings.ActivityDisabled: {activity} is currently disabled by the GM.
Warnings.ActivityPermission: You do not have permission to use this activity.
Warnings.InvalidRequest: The socket activity request is no longer valid.
Warnings.NoActiveGm: An active GM is required for socket activities.
Warnings.RequestFailed: Could not request a GM-mediated socket operation: {error}

SocketSlot.Title: Socket Slot
SocketSlot.Hint: Select the next clicked item to add a configured socket, or remove one empty slot from the current item.

Operation.Label: Operation
Operation.Hint: Choose whether this activity targets the next clicked item for a new socket or removes one empty slot from the current item.

IgnoreMaxSockets.Label: Ignore socket limit
IgnoreMaxSockets.Hint: When enabled, this activity can add a socket even if the item is already at the world socket limit.

TargetCondition.Label: Target item condition
TargetCondition.Hint: Use JavaScript to decide whether the clicked item can receive this socket. Available variables: item, targetItem, hostItem, actor, user, socketCount, game, getProperty, hasProperty, deepClone.

Fields.RemoveHint: This activity prompts for one empty slot on the item and removes it.

Warnings.AddFailed: Could not add a socket to {item}.
Warnings.NoEmptySlots: This item has no empty slots to remove.
Warnings.RemoveFailed: Could not remove the selected empty slot.
Warnings.SlotNotEmpty: Only empty slots can be removed with this activity.
Warnings.TargetConditionError: The target item condition for this socket activity could not be evaluated.
Warnings.TargetConditionFailed: The clicked item does not match the target condition for this socket activity.
Warnings.TargetConditionInvalid: The target item condition has invalid code.

SocketExtraction.Title: Socket Extraction
SocketExtraction.Hint: Use the activity, then click the target item. The activity removes one socketed gem and decides whether it is kept or deleted.

Fields.TargetingHint: When used, this activity targets the next clicked item. The selected result below overrides global and slot removal settings.
Fields.TargetingHintSelf: When used, this activity targets the item that owns it. The selected result below overrides global and slot removal settings.

TargetMode.Label: Target item
TargetMode.Hint: Choose whether the user clicks the item whose gem is removed or the activity extracts from the item that owns it.

Mode.Label: Removed Gem Outcome
Mode.Hint: Choose whether the removed gem is returned or destroyed. This ignores global and slot removal settings.
Mode.HintSelf: Choose whether the removed gem is returned or destroyed when extracting from the item that owns this activity. This ignores global and slot removal settings.

Selection.Prompt: Click the item whose socketed gem should be removed. Press Esc to cancel.

Dialogs.ConfirmBodyDelete: Use {name} as the target and delete the removed gem?
Dialogs.ConfirmBodyKeep: Use {name} as the target and keep the removed gem?
Dialogs.ItemPermissionBody: You do not have permission to edit {name}.
Dialogs.NoSocketedGemsBody: {name} has no socketed gems to remove.
Dialogs.OverrideHint: This activity ignores global and slot removal settings.

Warnings.EmptySlot: The selected slot does not contain a gem.
Warnings.ExtractionFailed: Could not extract the selected gem.
Warnings.NoSocketedGems: The target item has no socketed gems to remove.

Info.Deleted: The activity removed a socketed gem from {item} and destroyed it.
Info.Extracted: The activity removed a socketed gem from {item} and kept it.

App.Subtitle: {item} has {count} socketed gems. Choose which one to remove.
App.Title: Choose Gem to Remove

GemReload.Title: Gem Reload
GemReload.Hint: Use the activity, click the target item, then socket a compatible gem from the user's inventory into one of its empty sockets.

Fields.TargetingHint: When used, this activity targets the next clicked socketable item, or an item that already has sockets, then selects a compatible gem from the user's inventory and sockets it into an empty slot.
Fields.TargetingHintSelf: When used, this activity targets the item that owns it, then selects a compatible gem from the user's inventory and sockets it into one of its empty slots.

TargetMode.Label: Target item
TargetMode.Hint: Choose whether the user clicks the item that receives the gem or the activity reloads the item that owns it.

GemMode.Label: Gem selection
GemMode.Hint: Choose whether the user picks a compatible gem or the activity auto-selects one by exact name or match pattern.

GemQuery.Label: Gem filter
GemQuery.HintName: Case-insensitive exact gem name to auto-select from the actor inventory.
GemQuery.HintMatch: Case-insensitive gem name pattern to auto-select from the actor inventory. Use * as a wildcard.

SlotMode.Label: Slot selection
SlotMode.Hint: Choose whether the first compatible empty slot is used automatically or the user picks a slot.

Selection.Prompt: Click the item that should receive a gem. Press Esc to cancel.

Dialogs.ItemPermissionBody: You do not have permission to edit {name}.
Dialogs.SlotConditionErrorBody: Gem Reload could not evaluate one or more socket conditions on {name}: {slots}. Fix the slot condition or choose another item.

Warnings.GemNotFound: The selected gem is no longer available in the source actor inventory.
Warnings.InvalidGemQuery: Configure a gem name or match pattern before using this activity.
Warnings.NoCompatibleGems: No compatible gems are available for the selected target.
Warnings.NoEmptySlots: The target item has no empty slots available.
Warnings.NoSourceActor: This activity needs an actor inventory to draw gems from.
Warnings.SlotConditionError: Some sockets on {item} were skipped because their slot conditions could not be evaluated: {slots}.
Warnings.SlotOccupied: The selected socket is already occupied.
Warnings.SocketFailed: Could not socket the selected gem.

App.GemFilterNoResults: No gems match the current filter.
App.GemSubtitle: {actor} has {count} compatible gems for {item}. Choose which one to socket.
App.SlotSubtitle: {item} has {count} compatible empty slots for {gem}. Choose one.

SocketRecharge.Title: Socket Recharge
SocketRecharge.Hint: Use the activity, then click the target item and choose a socketed gem to restore its charges. Can require a check and roll the restored amount.

Fields.TargetingHint: When used, this activity targets the next clicked item and restores charges to one of its socketed gems.

Formula.Label: Recharge formula
Formula.Hint: Dice formula rolled to determine how many charges are restored (supports actor roll data, e.g. 1d4 + @abilities.int.mod). Leave blank to fully recharge the gem.

Type.Label: Required check
Type.Hint: Optionally require an ability check or skill check before the recharge. On a failure no charges are restored.

Dc.Label: Check DC
Dc.Hint: The difficulty class the check must meet or exceed for the recharge to succeed. Accepts a flat number or a deterministic formula using the actor's roll data (e.g. 8 + @prof or 10 + @abilities.int.mod).

ResourceKey.Label: Resource filter
ResourceKey.Hint: Optional charge name. When set, only socketed gems with this resource are offered for recharging. Leave blank to allow any gem with missing charges.

Selection.Prompt: Click the item whose socketed gem should be recharged. Press Esc to cancel.

Dialogs.ItemPermissionBody: You do not have permission to edit {name}.
Dialogs.NoMatchingGemsBody: {name} has no socketed gems with missing "{key}" charges.
Dialogs.NoRechargeableGemsBody: {name} has no socketed gems with missing charges.

Warnings.CheckFailed: The recharge attempt failed. No charges were restored.
Warnings.InvalidDcFormula: The check DC formula "{formula}" could not be resolved; using DC 10.
Warnings.InvalidFormula: The recharge formula "{formula}" could not be rolled.
Warnings.NoActor: This activity requires an actor to roll the recharge check.
Warnings.NoRechargeableGems: The target item has no socketed gems with missing charges.
Warnings.NothingRestored: The recharge roll did not restore any charges.

App.Subtitle: {item} has {count} rechargeable gems. Choose which one to recharge.
App.Title: Choose Gem to Recharge

SocketPoolRecharge.Title: Socket Pool Recharge
SocketPoolRecharge.Hint: Use the activity, then click the target item and choose one of its charge pools. The restored charges are spread across every socketed gem that provides that resource, in slot order.

Fields.TargetingHint: When used, this activity targets the next clicked item and restores charges to a whole resource pool, distributing them across its socketed gems in slot order.

Formula.Hint: Dice formula rolled to determine how many charges are restored across the pool (supports actor roll data). Leave blank to fully recharge the pool.

ResourceKey.Hint: Optional charge name. When set, this activity always recharges that pool. Leave blank to let the user choose among the item's pools with missing charges.

Selection.Prompt: Click the item whose socketed charge pool should be recharged. Press Esc to cancel.

Dialogs.NoPoolsBody: {name} has no socketed charge pools with missing charges.

Warnings.AlreadyFull: The "{key}" charge pool is already full.
Warnings.NoPool: The target item has no socketed "{key}" charges.

App.Subtitle: {item} has {count} charge pools with missing charges. Choose which one to recharge.
App.Title: Choose Charge Pool

SocketPools.Label: Resources
SocketPools.Hint: Aggregated resource pools provided by the socketed gems. The item itself stores no charges.

Consumption.FromHintCharge: Item recovery does not automatically restore socketed charges.
Consumption.FromHintGem: Item recovery does not automatically restore consumed socketed gems.

Filter.Label: Host item filter
Filter.Hint: Optional JavaScript condition. Only socketed host items for which it returns true contribute to this pool. Available variables: item, hostItem, sourceItem, actor, activity, user, game, getProperty, and hasProperty.
Filter.Invalid: The host item filter contains invalid JavaScript.
Filter.Error: The socket consumption item filter could not be evaluated: {error}
